conditions: Effect depends critically on stem ripeness. Ripe, lignified (brown/woody) stems contribute fine, structural tannins with a savoury, spicy character. Unripe, green stems contribute harsh, bitter, astringent tannins with green/vegetal notes. Magnitude scales with the percentage of whole bunch inclusion.
explanation: Lignified stem wood contains condensed tannins that are extracted by the fermenting must's ethanol over the course of fermentation. The texture of stem tannins is typically described as fine-grained, linear, or crunchy — distinct from the rounder, plumper texture of grape-skin tannins. Whole bunch also promotes partial carbonic maceration in intact berries (intracellular fermentation before berry burst), which generates glycerol and distinctive red-fruit/spice aromas alongside the tannin contribution.
